citation_short: IACtHR, Rodríguez Vera et al. v. Colombia (2014)
title: Case of Rodríguez Vera et al. (The Disappeared from the Palace of Justice) v. Colombia
pages_or_sections: paras. 93-102, pp. 33-35; disappearance discussion around p. 69
strengths: Judicial synthesis with detailed fact section and explicit participation count for the M-19 operation.
limitations: Not a clean final casualty ledger; identification and disappearance controversies remain unresolved.
notes: Useful for combatant presence and bounded-site description.

title: Hechos - Memoria Viva
pages_or_sections: section 'Los hechos ocurridos los días 6 y 7 de noviembre de 1985'
strengths: Official institutional summary with clear entry sequence of the assault.
limitations: Not designed as a denominator or casualty spreadsheet.

title: Víctimas - Memoria Viva
strengths: Explicitly warns that total deaths remain contested and identifies why clean totals are difficult.
limitations: Does not provide a single accepted tripartite direct-death split.
notes: Used to keep uncertainty visible rather than to set the main DR numerator.

title: Secuencia didáctica sobre historia del conflicto colombiano
strengths: One of the few sources in this wave giving a clean civilian/soldier/guerrilla split in one line.
limitations: Brief pedagogical source; should be treated as a contingent categorical split rather than an uncontested final ledger.
notes: This source underpins the publishable direct-death split used for the formal DR.

citation_short: Museo de la Independencia, 'El palacio arde'
title: El palacio arde
author_or_org: Museo de la Independencia Casa del Florero
strengths: Compact official summary with a usable hostages count and approximate state-force scale.
limitations: Also gives an alternative overall fatality total that conflicts with other sources.
notes: Used for bounded exposure counts, not for the main death split.

title: 6 de noviembre de 1985: toma del Palacio de Justicia
pages_or_sections: paras. on the retaking force and the building destroyed after 28 hours of combat
strengths: Provides the simplest explicit duration statement for person-month conversion.
limitations: Secondary historical summary rather than a case file.
notes: Used only for duration and troop-scale corroboration.
